taxonomy: Camaridium aurantiacum, also known as the orange orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Central and South America and is known for its vibrant orange flowers. Within the Orchidaceae family, Camaridium aurantiacum is classified as part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Camaridium is considered a synonym for the genus Cattleya, which is a group of large and showy orchids. As a result, Camaridium aurantiacum is also classified as Cattleya aurantiaca.
uses: Camaridium aurantiacum, also known as the Orange Air Plant, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
